content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 23,
1993
Kara Kobey
3760 Hancock Street
Suite H
San Diego,
CA
92110
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—93—426
Dear Ms. Kobey:
I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
December 13,
1993, wherein I advised you that the Kobey
Corporation’s sponsorship of the “Octoberfest” celebration in
October of 1994 would be considered a non—monetary or “in—kind”
contribution to you from the Kobey Corporation.
You are the Vice
President for Community Affairs of the Kobey Corporation and serve
as a voting member of the board of directors.
You are also a
member of the San Diego Community College Board of Trustees and
will be a candidate for re-election in November of 1994.
In October of 1994, the Kobey Corporation is planning to
sponsor an “Octoberfest” celebration, with a portion of the
proceeds to be donated to a designated charity.
Any advertising
would refer to the Kobey Corporation as “The Kobey Corporation,”
“Kobey’s” or “Kobey’s Swap Meet at the Sports Arena.”
You stated
that all three forms of the name of the corporation are widely
known in San Diego.
The name of the charity will also be included
in the advertising.
Proceeds from the Octoberfest will be donated to the San
Diego Mesa College Foundation,
a 501(c) (3)
organization under the
Internal Revenue Code, which provides independent financial sup
port for Mesa College.
This college is one of the campuses
governed by the San Diego Community College Board of Trustees,
of
which you are a member.
I
am forwarding a copy of Regulation 18215, which defines the
term “contribution,” and Advice Letter No. A-90-163, which
includes a discussion of in—kind contributions.
Any contribution
to you is subject to the reporting requirements of the Political
Reform Act.
You also asked if a donation from the Kobey Corporation to a
local AIDS organization would be considered an in-kind
contribution to you.
I advised you and Mr. Jackson, your consult
ant, that we would need more facts regarding the nature of the
donation.
Please submit the information in a written request so
we may respond to you.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807
•
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—93—426
Page 2
I trust this answers your question.
SGC: JS: ak
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Legal Division
Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Kobey Corporation's sponsorship of an 'Octoberfest' celebration in October 1994, with proceeds donated to the San Diego Mesa College Foundation, constitutes an in-kind contribution to Ms. Kobey, a Vice President and board member of the corporation who also serves on the San Diego Community College Board of Trustees.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the Kobey Corporation's sponsorship of the Octoberfest celebration would be considered a non-monetary or 'in-kind' contribution to Ms. Kobey and is subject to the reporting requirements of the Political Reform Act. The FPPC requests additional written information regarding a separate proposed donation to a local AIDS organization before responding to that inquiry.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Letter format advice letter confirming telephone conversation from December 13, 1993. No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections present, but clearly structured as FPPC response to advice request. Enclosures referenced (Regulation 18215 and Advice Letter A-90-163) not included in document text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Kobey Corporation's sponsorship of an 'Octoberfest' celebration in October 1994, with proceeds donated to the San Diego Mesa College Foundation, constitutes an in-kind contribution to Ms. Kobey, a Vice President and board member of the corporation who also serves on the San Diego Community College Board of Trustees.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advises that the Kobey Corporation's sponsorship of the Octoberfest celebration would be considered a non-monetary or 'in-kind' contribution to Ms. Kobey and is subject to the reporting requirements of the Political Reform Act. The FPPC requests additional written information regarding a separate proposed donation to a local AIDS organization before responding to that inquiry.
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission December 23, 1993 Kara Kobey 3760 Hancock Street Suite H San Diego, CA 92110 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A—93—426 Dear Ms. Kobey: I am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on December 13, 1993, wherein I advised you that the Kobey Corporation’s sponsorship of the “Octoberfest” celebration in October of 1994 would be considered a non—monetary or “in—kind” contribution to you from the Kobey Corporation. You are the Vice President for Community Affairs of the Kobey Corporation and serve as a voting member of the board of directors. You are also a member of the San Diego Community College Board of Trustees and will be a candidate for re-election in November of 1994. In October of 1994, the Kobey Corporation is planning to sponsor an “Octoberfest” celebration, with a portion of the proceeds to be donated to a designated charity. Any advertising would refer to the Kobey Corporation as “The Kobey Corporation,” “Kobey’s” or “Kobey’s Swap Meet at the Sports Arena.” You stated that all three forms of the name of the corporation are widely known in San Diego. The name of the charity will also be included in the advertising. Proceeds from the Octoberfest will be donated to the San Diego Mesa College Foundation, a 501(c) (3) organization under the Internal Revenue Code, which provides independent financial sup port for Mesa College. This college is one of the campuses governed by the San Diego Community College Board of Trustees, of which you are a member. I am forwarding a copy of Regulation 18215, which defines the term “contribution,” and Advice Letter No. A-90-163, which includes a discussion of in—kind contributions. Any contribution to you is subject to the reporting requirements of the Political Reform Act. You also asked if a donation from the Kobey Corporation to a local AIDS organization would be considered an in-kind contribution to you. I advised you and Mr. Jackson, your consult ant, that we would need more facts regarding the nature of the donation. Please submit the information in a written request so we may respond to you. 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 File No. A—93—426 Page 2 I trust this answers your question. SGC: JS: ak Sincerely, Steven G. Churchwell General Counsel By: Legal Division Enclosures
embedding.summary: FPPC General Counsel confirms that the Kobey Corporation's sponsorship of an Octoberfest celebration with proceeds to San Diego Mesa College Foundation constitutes an in-kind contribution to Ms. Kobey, a board member of both the corporation and the Community College District, and is subject to Political Reform Act reporting requirements.
